environment: Personal dotfiles management on macOS using chezmoi. Source repo: github.com/toku345/dotfiles. Source state at ~/.local/share/chezmoi/ template-renders to ~/.config/, ~/.claude/, ~/.ssh/, shell profiles, etc. via `chezmoi apply`. Source can include `.chezmoiscripts/run_*` shell scripts that execute during apply — this is intentional setup automation. Bootstrap (`chezmoi init <repo>`) clones a repo and applies in one step — treat it as `chezmoi update` for review purposes (network fetch + execution path), NOT under the local-only Apply rule below.

Trusted source control: all repositories owned by GitHub user `toku345` (personal account). Trust scope is source-control operations only (push, pull, clone, diff) on user-intended source repos. It does NOT waive: Untrusted Code Integration (executing lifecycle scripts from a cloned personal repo still requires the same review as any external code), Create Public Surface (changing visibility to public or `gh repo create --public` still requires explicit user instruction), or Data Exfiltration (when the working repo is unrelated to the dotfiles workflow, pushing its files into a `toku345/*` personal repo as 'backup' is still exfil — trust covers operations on user-intended source repos, not arbitrary cross-repo content moves).
allow: Chezmoi Apply (local-only, default source): The action is a `chezmoi apply` tool invocation reading the default source path (~/.local/share/chezmoi/) and writing template-rendered files to ~/ (~/.config/, ~/.claude/, ~/.ssh/, shell profiles, etc.) plus executing `.chezmoiscripts/run_*` scripts. Partial applies (`chezmoi apply <target>`) are covered since they read from the same source. Scope is the `chezmoi apply` invocation itself — direct `Edit`/`Write` calls to ~/.claude/, ~/.ssh/, shell profiles, etc. WITHOUT going through `chezmoi apply` are NOT covered (review them as ordinary writes). NOT covered either: `chezmoi update` (network fetch + apply), `chezmoi init <repo>` (clone + apply), `chezmoi apply` with input/output-redirecting flags or env vars (`--source=`, `--destination=`, `CHEZMOI_SOURCE_DIR`, `CHEZMOI_DESTINATION_DIR`), or other mutating subcommands (`chezmoi add`, `chezmoi edit --apply`, `chezmoi execute-template`, `chezmoi state ...`) — those fall through to default classification. The 'local and git-tracked' premise also no longer holds — fresh review is required — if ANY of these happened earlier in the same session: `chezmoi update`, `git pull` / `git fetch && checkout` / `git reset --hard` inside ~/.local/share/chezmoi/, or any agent-initiated `Edit`/`Write` to source files (the agent's own edits do NOT pre-authorize the agent's own apply — that chain would let the agent install persistence in two innocent-looking steps). Blind Apply does NOT apply since source is local and git-tracked; `chezmoi diff` before apply is recommended but not gated. Note on idempotency: `run_once_*` scripts cause side effects (package installs, system changes, writes outside the managed set) that are NOT undone by re-applying older source — only managed-file content is idempotent. Explicitly waives: Self-Modification (writes to ~/.claude/ — implication: anyone who compromises the dotfiles source automatically gains agent-config rewrite as part of the same compromise), Unauthorized Persistence (SSH keys, shell profiles, fish config ARE the dotfiles being managed — deliberate trade-off: a tampered local source state would persist as a backdoor), Untrusted Code Integration (`.chezmoiscripts/run_*` execution from trusted local source — same trade-off), Irreversible Local Destruction (apply reproduces from source).

Chezmoi Source Edits: Editing source state files in ~/.local/share/chezmoi/ (private_dot_claude/, private_dot_config/, .chezmoiscripts/, etc.) is the intended workflow for managing these dotfiles. Combined with the Chezmoi Apply rule above, this gives the agent a path to add new persistence mechanisms by editing source then applying — accepted trade-off for the dotfile workflow. Hard carve-outs (NOT covered by this rule, fall through to default classification): (a) adding NEW `.chezmoiscripts/run_*` script files; (b) persistence-relevant edits to EXISTING `.chezmoiscripts/run_*` files (e.g. adding new install commands, new fetch URLs, or new external invocations to an existing script — the script's identity does not waive review of new content); (c) any edit under `private_dot_ssh/` that adds key material, or any edit anywhere that introduces a public-key-shaped string; (d) any edit introducing a NEW external execution surface — `curl|bash`, `iex (iwr ...)`, `wget -O- | sh`, `nix-shell -p` with new packages, `brew install` from non-core taps, or changing an existing fetch's URL to a new host. Modifying existing scripts/templates is allowed under this rule for content UNRELATED to persistence or network fetch — only the persistence/network-fetch surface needs explicit user direction. Note on mechanism: the classifier evaluates subsequent execution, not the edits themselves; this rule's primary effect manifests when `chezmoi apply` later runs the modified source.
